executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware crisis with 103+ organizations breached in the past 48 hours across all critical sectors. High-profile victims include Canada Goose, National Money Mart (database leaked), Eastern Townships School Board, Peterborough Public Health, and Municipality of North Perth. The Qilin ransomware group leads with 20+ Canadian victims, followed by Play, Clop, and SafePay gangs. Additionally, CCCS issued Alert AL26-009 for CVE-2026-31431 affecting Linux systems. This represents a coordinated national-scale attack requiring immediate response.

items.description: Vulnerability affecting Linux systems - details not disclosed in alert

items.name: Qilin
items.targets: 22 Canadian organizations including Canada Goose, Eastern Townships School Board, Kuzco Lighting, Waterloo Information Systems, Traffic Tech, NovAtel (Hexagon), BioPharma Services, Workers Health & Safety Centre, and multiple SMBs across manufacturing, construction, technology, and professional services
items.ttps: T1486 Data Encrypted for Impact, T1567 Exfiltration Over Web Service, T1490 Inhibit System Recovery - double extortion ransomware with data leak site

items.name: Play
items.targets: 11 Canadian organizations including Equine Canada, Chemical Computing Group, Paisley Products, Makivik, ALLMAX, Select Tool, Security ONE Alarm Systems, Katch Kan, and Turkstra Trusses
items.ttps: T1486 Data Encrypted for Impact, T1059 Command and Scripting Interpreter, T1490 Inhibit System Recovery

items.name: Clop
items.targets: 13 Canadian organizations including law firms (SMT Lawyers), technology firms (Cloud Data Works, Sensible Solutions, Technikel, ModTech), energy (East Plats), hospitality (Granite Pointe, Whiskijack Resorts), and business services
items.ttps: T1486 Data Encrypted for Impact, T1190 Exploit Public-Facing Application, T1078 Valid Accounts - known for exploiting file transfer vulnerabilities

items.name: SafePay
items.targets: 10 Canadian organizations including Teccart education, construction firms (Groupe Piche, Kenalex, Silverline Group), manufacturing (Precision Aluminum), agriculture (Matt and Steve, Ripobec), and transportation (Briway Carriers)
items.ttps: T1486 Data Encrypted for Impact, T1567 Exfiltration Over Web Service

items.name: TheGentlemen
items.targets: 5 Canadian organizations including Labtician Ophthalmics (Healthcare), Groupe Caddac (Construction), ControlGMC (Manufacturing), Real Tech, and Colliers
items.ttps: T1486 Data Encrypted for Impact, T1213 Data from Information Repositories

items.name: IncRansom
items.targets: 8 Canadian organizations including healthcare (Bison Family Medical), manufacturing (CPK Interior, Steelworks, Omega Tool Corp), construction (Excavation Tourigny), education (Bais Yaakov), financial services (Reidhurst Nagy), and consumer services (Glassers TV)
items.ttps: T1486 Data Encrypted for Impact, T1490 Inhibit System Recovery

items.name: DragonForce
items.targets: 6 Canadian organizations including Phoenix Labs, CCR Solutions, Esposito Bros. Construction, Soteck, McKee-Pownall Equine Services, and Emond Publishing
items.ttps: T1486 Data Encrypted for Impact, T1003 OS Credential Dumping, T1021 Remote Services

items.name: Lynx
items.targets: 5 Canadian organizations including Peterborough Public Health (critical infrastructure), Trucash (Financial Services), KTL Group, Laurysen Kitchens, and Group Echo
items.ttps: T1486 Data Encrypted for Impact, T1078 Valid Accounts

items.name: Sinobi
items.targets: 3 Canadian organizations including Kids & Company (childcare), Active Green + Ross (automotive), and Morison Insurance Brokers
items.ttps: T1486 Data Encrypted for Impact, T1567 Exfiltration Over Web Service

items.name: AiLock
items.targets: 2 Canadian organizations: Solutions Extreme Technology and Eeyou Communications Network (Telecommunication)

items.name: Devman
items.targets: 4 Canadian organizations including Axion50plus (Financial Services), Ravand (Technology), and Beausejour Co-op (Agriculture)

items.name: Nightspire
items.targets: 2 Canadian organizations: Regal Building Materials and an undisclosed law firm

items.name: Everest
items.targets: National Money Mart Company with database leaked - major financial services breach
items.ttps: T1486 Data Encrypted for Impact, T1567 Exfiltration Over Web Service, T1530 Data from Cloud Storage - confirmed data exfiltration

canada_landscape.summary: Canada faces a national-scale ransomware emergency with 103+ confirmed victims across all provinces and sectors in 48 hours. CCCS issued Alert AL26-009 (Update 1) for CVE-2026-31431 affecting Linux systems. Critical infrastructure compromised includes municipal governments (North Perth, MRC de Maskinongé), public health (Peterborough), education (Eastern Townships School Board, Teccart), and telecommunications (Eeyou Communications Network).
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose (major apparel retailer) compromised by CoinbaseCartel ransomware gang; Active Green + Ross (automotive retail) hit by Sinobi
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company suffered major breach by Everest gang with database leaked; Morison Insurance Brokers hit by Sinobi; Trucash compromised by Lynx; multiple accounting firms targeted including Pewarchuk CPA, MG CPA, and Reidhurst Nagy

report_2w.executive_summary: CISA has added actively exploited ConnectWise and Windows vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog, requiring immediate patching. A critical CVSS 10.0 vulnerability in Google's Gemini CLI allows remote code execution. SAP's official npm packages were compromised in a supply chain attack dubbed 'Mini Shai Hulud,' exfiltrating developer credentials and CI/CD secrets. The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security issued three alerts in the past week, including warnings about social engineering attacks on enterprise SaaS environments and critical vulnerabilities in cPanel and Cisco devices. An unverified claim suggests Mitsubishi Canada Aerospace may have been breached by an actor using the 'Ryuk' moniker.

items.cia_impact: Confidentiality: High, Integrity: High, Availability: Low

items.ttps: T1195.002 (Compromise Software Supply Chain), credential harvesting, preinstall scripts, obfuscated code execution

canada_landscape.summary: The Canadian Centre for Cyber Security (CCCS) issued three alerts in the past week: AL26-010 on social engineering compromises of enterprise SaaS environments, AL26-008 on a critical cPanel/WHM vulnerability (CVE-2026-41940), and an update on Cisco ASA/FTD vulnerabilities. An unverified claim alleges that Mitsubishi Canada Aerospace was breached by an actor using the 'Ryuk' moniker. No incidents involving major Canadian retailers or financial institutions were identified.
